description: Monarchs, nobles, and members of the royal court.
jobs: Emperor
A scepter-holding sovereign ruling an empire of many kingdoms from the highest throne.
Empress
A crown-bearing ruler holding supreme authority over an empire in her own right.
King
A crown-wearing sovereign governing a kingdom from the throne room.
Queen
A throne-seated ruler commanding a kingdom as sovereign or crowned consort.
High King
A paramount authority binding rival crowns under a single, unquestioned rule.
High Queen
A supreme sovereign reigning over queens and kingdoms alike from the paramount throne.
Prince
A sword-training heir studying statecraft and arms in the shadow of the crown.
Princess
A court-raised daughter of the crown studying diplomacy and the ways of rule.
Archduke
A near-royal governor of an archduchy, ranking just below the crown in the peerage.
Archduchess
A high-ranking administrator presiding beside the archduke at court.
Grand Duke
A sovereign-territory ruler outranking an ordinary duke by charter and tradition.
Grand Duchess
A grand-duchy holder wielding authority in her own name or by right of marriage.
Duke
A duchy-commanding lord marshaling armies, levying taxes, and holding courts beneath the crown.
Duchess
A silk-and-steel steward overseeing the affairs and lands of a duchy at court.
Marquis
A border-guarding lord governing the wild marches where the kingdom meets untamed land.
Marchioness
A frontier-estate manager balancing border defense with the social affairs of a marquisate.
Count
A county-governing noble collecting taxes, hearing disputes, and keeping order.
Countess
A land-stewarding noblewoman representing a county's interests at the royal court.
Earl
A shire-mustering governor overseeing levies, justice, and local affairs.
Viscount
A deputy administrator managing a portion of a county on the count's behalf.
Viscountess
An estate-managing noblewoman handling social duties for the viscountcy.
Baron
A small-fief holder -- the lowest titled noble, holding land directly from the crown.
Baroness
A household-running noblewoman managing the daily affairs of a barony.
Baronet
A hereditary-title bearer with gentle standing but no seat in the upper peerage.
Knight
A plate-armored rider sworn by oath to serve lord and crown on the battlefield.
Dame
A valor-earned title bearer, knighted for service or courage in defense of the realm.
Lord
A rent-collecting landowner dispensing low justice to the tenants on his holdings.
Lady
A household-managing noblewoman representing the family name at court.
Esquire
A gentle-born landholder ranked above commoners but below the knights.
Gentleman
A rent-living member of the gentry, the lowest rung of gentle society.
Squire
A shield-carrying youth learning the ways of chivalry at a knight's side.
Page
A table-serving child learning etiquette and duty as the first step toward knighthood.
Regent
A crown-wielding stand-in governing on behalf of an absent or underage monarch.
Heir Apparent
A statecraft-groomed successor standing next in line to inherit the throne.
Consort
A ceremonial partner sharing the monarch's life and duties without ruling power.
Viceroy
A distant-province ruler governing in the monarch's name and likeness.
Page Boy
A corridor-running youth delivering messages and errands through the manor's halls.
Ladies in Waiting
A circle of attendants managing the queen's wardrobe, correspondence, and companionship.
Lady in Waiting
A close-attending companion dressing, advising, and accompanying a noblewoman through court life.
